event.name: Electric Forest
event.earlyArrival: Standard GA campers arrive Thursday June 25 (Wednesday June 24 = +$50 add-on, one per campsite/vehicle). GA Preferred Car Camping, GA RV/Powered RV, Lucky Lake Pre-Set RV, Group Camping include Wednesday arrival. All Good Life tiers include Wednesday June 24, 8:00 AM ET. Back 40 camping/FHU/Enchanted: Wednesday June 24, 8:00 AM ET; Back 40 Cabins/Log Homes: Wednesday June 24, 3:00 PM ET. Hotel packages: 5 nights, check-in Wed June 24 / check-out Mon June 29.
event.boxOffice: Winston Speedway Box Office (new location for 2026) opens Tuesday June 23 at 10 PM and runs 24 hrs through the weekend; all will-call must be picked up there before reaching the toll entrances.
event.source: electricforest.com/pass-types; Wikipedia (14th edition); research compiled June 18, 2026

weather.climatology: Late-June Rothbury (49452): typical highs ~74–78°F, lows ~55–60°F; June day-highs 68–88°F, lows 52–69°F; ~14 rain days/month totaling ~2.5"; humidity ~80%; UV index ~5; ~15.5 hrs daylight (sunrise ~6:05 AM, sunset ~9:30 PM); no snow May–September. The severe-storm pattern is real and recurring.
weather.packFor: Plan for warm, humid days near 80°F, cool nights in the mid-to-upper 50s, and a high likelihood of at least one thunderstorm. Bring sun protection (SPF, hat, sunglasses, bandana), rain protection (poncho/rain jacket, pack-cover, extra tarp, dry bag), a warm layer/hoodie for nights, sturdy closed-toe shoes plus a rinse-off pair, and a documented severe-weather plan.
weather.stormNote: In a storm, shelter in a hard vehicle or a shuttle/shelter bus — NOT a tent (Michigan State Police: "do not try to shelter in your tent. Go into your vehicle"). Stake/guy-out everything; secure pop-ups, tarps, and loose items. Monitor the EF app and 99.5 FM. The 2024 festival evacuated twice and 2025 also saw weather disruptions.
weather.sources: Synthesized from weather.com, FOX Weather, and NWS Grand Rapids (GRR), pulled June 17–18, 2026; AccuWeather and weather-us.com for climatology.

everywhere: Grills, open flame, candles, charcoal (small Coleman-style camp stoves with ≤1 lb propane canisters are the only exemption)
Generators (RV built-in gas generators and solar generators OK; external gas/diesel/kerosene generators and any gas/fuel cans banned)
Speakers larger than 11x5x5 (handheld/USB personal speakers OK)
venueAdditional: Chairs, lawn, or beach furniture (no-pump inflatable WindPouch-type seating allowed, except on the Ranch Arena lawn)
Large markers, paint pens, spray paint
permittedDaypack: Small non-framed bag, fanny pack, or small hydration pack
Empty reusable bottle/hydration pack (or one factory-sealed water bottle ≤1 gallon)
Small handmade gifts, pins, kandi for trading
Totem ≤7 ft on light PVC (no wood/metal, nothing sharp on top, no threatening messaging)
Factory-sealed single-dose naloxone nasal spray (4 mg)
prohibited.alcohol: Campgrounds, 21+: one case of beer/seltzer OR one box of wine per person; no glass, no liquor, no kegs; cannot be carried into the venue.
prohibited.meds: Keep prescriptions in original labeled bottles; ID must match; declare at search.
prohibited.harmReduction: Bunk Police will NOT be on-site in 2026 — source any fentanyl/xylazine test strips and reagent/purity kits before arrival; keep prescriptions labeled; factory-sealed single-dose naloxone nasal spray is permitted.

providesVocab.tent: Shelter (pre-pitched tent or hard-sided unit) provided — no need to bring your own tent or stakes/mallet.
providesVocab.sleeping-gear: Sleeping bags and/or sleeping pads/cots provided.
providesVocab.bedding: Real bed(s) with sheets, pillows, and comforters/blankets provided.
providesVocab.cooling: Cooling unit / air conditioning provided in your lodging.
providesVocab.furniture: Camp furniture provided (side tables, chairs, and/or bistro table).
providesVocab.lighting: In-unit lighting provided (lanterns or battery light).
providesVocab.lighting-partial: Only a single battery light is provided — bring an extra lantern.
providesVocab.tent-lock: A lock for the tent/unit is provided.
providesVocab.soap: Soap / dish soap / toilet paper provided.
providesVocab.comp-showers: Complimentary showers + A/C restrooms provided (no shower-haul needed, but personal toiletries still required).
providesVocab.shared-showers: Shared shower facilities available (basic, not A/C).
providesVocab.concierge: On-site concierge / Good Life base perks (A/C lounge, charging, dedicated entrances).
providesVocab.parking-pass: A parking/car pass is included with this option.
providesVocab.car-onsite: You camp beside your car (car/companion pass included; pack heavier).
providesVocab.power-30a: 30A electrical service provided at the site.
providesVocab.full-hookup: Full hook-up provided: 30A electrical + water + sewer (no daily services or water-rationing needed).
providesVocab.in-tent-outlet: A single in-tent outlet is provided, rated for charging small electronic devices only.
providesVocab.hotel-room: Hotel room with linens, towels, A/C, housekeeping, and a real bathroom — no camping gear needed.
providesVocab.swim: Beach/pool/swim access included with this option.
providesVocab.in-camp-ice: In-camp ice sales available (lighter cooler-loading possible).
providesVocab.brunch: Daily brunch provided (Thu–Sun, 9 AM–1 PM ET) — reduces breakfast-food needs.
providesVocab.meal-card: $25 meal card per person + welcome basket included.
providesVocab.rv-bring: BYO RV option — you bring and run the RV.
providesVocab.linens-unconfirmed: EF does not confirm linens/towels for this lodging in writing — bring your own as backup or confirm with the Good Life concierge.

catalog.note: Name must match your prescription labels.
Register in the EF app BEFORE putting them on.
Exterior upper driver's-side windshield, before you arrive.
Venue is cashless; cash is for tolls, gas, off-site stops.

catalog.text: EF app + offline map & set times saved
catalog.note: Cell service degrades at peak (40k–50k people).

catalog.text: Printed directions + Will Call (Winston Speedway Box Office) location
catalog.note: Cell service is unreliable on-site.
Kept separate from your main key.

catalog.text: Written phone numbers + a group meetup spot/time

catalog.note: Metal hammers get confiscated — bring a rubber mallet.
Limited tree cover in most GA fields; stake and guy it out — pop-ups catch wind.

catalog.text: Sleeping bag + sleeping pad or air mattress

catalog.note: Provided sleeping bags/cots come without sheets — bring a liner.

catalog.text: Extra warm blanket or sleeping-bag liner
catalog.note: Nights hit the low-to-mid 50s; no throw blanket is included.
Comfort over the provided bed.

catalog.text: Your own sheets / blankets / pillows as backup
catalog.note: EF doesn't confirm linens for this unit — bring as backup or confirm with the Good Life concierge (goodlife@electricforestfestival.com).
Sun's up around 6 AM.

catalog.text: On arrival: confirm cooling-unit operation + nearest shower/shuttle

catalog.note: Peace of mind for gear in camp.
Buy ice at your last stop; locals sell out on EF weekend.
Low/no-cook options: bars, fruit, nut butter, wraps, jerky, oatmeal, pre-cooked protein.
≤1 lb propane only — grills, charcoal & open flame are banned.
Coffee maker is provided but coffee is not.
Both must be EMPTY to enter the venue.

catalog.text: Non-glass gallon jugs / drinking water for camp
catalog.note: Free refill stations throughout; bring drinking water beyond the daily refill for Pre-Set RVs.
Essential in the heat and humidity.

catalog.text: Day: breathable tops, shorts, tanks/dresses; extra socks + underwear

catalog.note: Genuinely cold after dark.
Cotton stays wet.

catalog.text: Bandana / neck gaiter, sun hat, sunglasses

catalog.note: Miles of uneven terrain.
So wet/muddy ones can dry.
Mud is real after storms.
For the beach/pool.
At least one, ideally a spare.
Separate towel for the beach/pool; dry bag keeps gear dry.
Chafing.
Bandages, blister pads, ointment, pain reliever, anti-diarrheal, antihistamine.
Name matches your ID.
Factory-sealed; explicitly permitted.
Bunk Police will NOT be on-site in 2026 — source any drug-checking supplies BEFORE arrival.
Reapply through the day.
Primarily for the venue and long charging lines; cell towers collapse with 40k–50k people.

catalog.text: Lanterns + LED string lights for camp

catalog.note: Only one battery light is included.
Cell towers collapse with 40k–50k people.

catalog.text: Chargers / batteries for LED gear + flow toys

catalog.note: Backup air; tents get hot.
Pre-set tents have no A/C and get hot — a battery/USB fan is essential.
Use the in-tent outlet for low-draw devices only — fan + phone charging + a small light is fine; do NOT run high-draw appliances (portable A/C, heaters).
Needed for 30A shore power.

catalog.text: Sewer hose + fittings, drinking-water hose + pressure regulator
catalog.note: Full hook-up: connect water and sewer.

catalog.text: Fresh-water fill + grey/black-water plan, leveling blocks, battery/solar power
catalog.note: Self-contained: no on-site dump; external gas generators banned (built-in/solar OK).
Every bag is searched; framed/large packs are banned in the venue.
Cashless venue.
Hearing protection at the bass stages.

catalog.text: LED accessories / kandi to trade; small flashlight

catalog.note: Chairs aren't allowed inside the venue.
Silver side OUT, with an air gap off the tent fabric. Cinch them down if weather turns; they catch wind like sails. For pre-set tents, check with the concierge that covering the tent is OK.

catalog.text: Outdoor rug / mat for the tent entrance

catalog.note: Personalize and find your camp.
Camp only — no knives in the venue.
Metal hammers get confiscated.

catalog.text: Duct tape; zip ties; jumper cables
Wagon / cart for the walk-in haul
catalog.note: Carts are allowed in the campground (banned only IN the venue).
Tie them down so they don't blow away.
Greywater to pod sinks — strain first.

catalog.text: Reusable shopping bag for ice / supply hauls
Plan to donate usable gear on the way out

catalog.note: For the pin-trading culture.
Giving Tree: handmade/meaningful only.

catalog.text: LED gear, glow accessories, flow toys
catalog.note: With chargers.
≤7 ft, light PVC (no wood/metal), nothing sharp on top.
Biodegradable glitter only.
No pro / detachable-lens cameras in the venue.

catalog.text: Hairbrush/comb, hair ties + bobby pins, compact mirror, makeup

catalog.note: Powder helps with chafe and damp feet.
High-draw heating appliance — only run it on real AC power (RV shore power, your A/C-unit circuit, or a hotel outlet). Do NOT run it off a pre-set tent’s charging-only outlet.

catalog.text: AC box fan + extension cord + power strip
catalog.note: Great airflow when you have power — A/C Landing runs one off the unit’s circuit / your own cord.

catalog.text: Playing cards / UNO / camp + car games

catalog.note: Sun and rain.
Keep the tent floor and site clear; pack out everything.
For the ≤1 lb propane camp stove; handy around camp.

catalog.text: Cooking tools: tongs, spatula, cutting board, skillet, foil
catalog.note: Camp only — knives/blades are banned inside the venue.
Double as phone waterproofing in rain.

catalog.text: Grab-and-go snacks (bars, jerky, trail mix, candy)
catalog.note: Easy daytime fuel that needs no cooking or cooler.

catalog.text: Drinks — cans/seltzers or boxed wine only (21+)
catalog.note: Campground 21+: one case of beer/seltzer OR one box of wine per person. No glass, no kegs, no liquor; none enters the venue.

catalog.text: Air pump for the air mattress
catalog.note: Walk-in sites may have no car power — bring a battery pump (or a 12V inflator if camping beside your car).
Handheld/USB only, under 11x5x5″ to clear venue rules; larger speakers are banned.

options.name: A/C Landing
options.lodging: Glamping tent with a real bed (1 queen or 2 twins) and cooling unit.
notes: Provided: tent, real bed, sheets/pillows/comforters, cooling unit, side table, battery light, tent lock, comp showers + A/C restrooms, 24-hr concierge, shuttle.
NOT provided: towels (bring your own) and chairs (bring a camp chair).
If reflective-tarping the pre-set tent, confirm with the concierge that covering it is OK.

options.name: GA Tent Camping
options.lodging: BYO tent in the GA campground.
notes: Everything — full master camping kit.
Buy a parking pass separately (or arrive with a multi-person car).
Bring extra shade — limited tree cover.

options.name: GA Car Camping
options.lodging: BYO tent beside your car; site ~12'×35'.
notes: Full master kit; pack heavier since the car is on-site (bigger cooler, more water, car-powered inflator).
Narrow site (~12 ft wide) — a low-profile canopy fits better than a large pavilion.

options.name: GA Preferred Car Camping
options.lodging: BYO tent beside your car, guaranteed site near main entrance; site ~20'×25' (new for 2026).
notes: Full master kit; the extra space supports a full canopy + larger group setup.
Guaranteed location near the main venue entrance regardless of arrival time.

options.name: GA RV
options.lodging: BYO RV; includes 1 companion car (<16 ft) pass. No electrical hookup.
notes: Run the RV self-contained — fresh water (no hookup), full propane for onboard systems, battery/solar power.
External gas generators banned; RV built-in gas generators and solar OK.
Leveling blocks, grey/black-water plan (no on-site dump). Bring a venue daypack.

options.name: GA Powered RV
options.lodging: BYO RV with 30A electrical + companion car.
notes: 30A power cord/adapter and 30A surge protector; otherwise self-contained water and waste (powered, not full hookup).
Run rooftop A/C off shore power. Fresh-water fill and a grey/black-water plan still needed.

options.name: GA Pre-Set Tent
options.lodging: Pre-pitched specially-designed tent + 2 sleeping bags + 2 sleeping pads (yours to keep). Formerly EFfortless.
notes: Bring pillow + sheet/liner, camp chair, shade/canopy if desired, cooler + ice, lantern/headlamp, power banks, toiletries + quick-dry towel + shower shoes, food + stove.
Bring a fan — no in-tent power; tents can get hot.
2026 tent design revealed on arrival.

options.name: GA Maplewoods
options.lodging: Forested/shaded walk-in BYO tent site.
notes: Full BYO tent master kit, but shade canopy is less critical (tree cover).
Bring a wagon/cart for the walk-in haul, extra rope/tarps for the wooded site, and bug spray (wooded).

options.name: GA Lucky Lake — Camping
options.lodging: Walk-in BYO tent; beach/swim access.
notes: Full BYO tent kit PLUS swim gear: swimsuit, beach/quick-dry towel, water shoes, dry bag, extra SPF, bug spray.
Walk-in haul cart. Ice can be bought in-camp (lighter cooler-loading possible).

options.name: GA Lucky Lake — Lakeside
options.lodging: BYO tent beside your car, prime lakefront location (car camping pass included).
notes: Same as Lucky Lake camping but pack heavier (car on-site); swim gear is the defining extra.
Very limited availability.

options.name: GA Lucky Lake — Pre-Set RV
options.lodging: Furnished Pre-Set RV: 30A, daily waste/water service Thu–Sun, queen + bunks, full kitchen, shower/toilet.
notes: Bring: food/groceries, drinking water beyond daily refill, your own coffee, personal toiletries, swim gear (swimsuit/towel/water shoes), venue daypack.
Don't bring a generator. Includes 1 car pass.

options.name: Camp Higher Love
options.lodging: BYO tent, larger sites; camp beside your car (1 car camping pass included).
notes: Full BYO tent master kit (camp next to car since car pass included).
Higher Love Lounge (shaded seating) + dedicated shuttle reduce the need to haul your own shade/seating to the venue, but you still need full camp gear.

options.name: Group Camping
options.lodging: BYO tent in designated group areas near Main Street.
notes: Full BYO tent master kit; group gear coordination (shared canopy, group flag/totem to find camp, communal water/shade).
Camp Traction = substance-free homebase (no alcohol); Her Forest = femme-identifying space; Wayfinder = guided/solo-friendly.
Parking/car pass per the specific group option.

options.name: GA Hotel Package
options.lodging: Hotel room (linens, towels, A/C, housekeeping, real bathroom); 5 nights, Wed check-in. ~Shoreline Inn (~27 mi).
options.arrival: Check-in Wed June 24 / check-out Mon June 29.
notes: Venue daypack only (empty bottle, power bank, poncho, warm layer, SPF, earplugs, ID/card), plus a normal hotel travel bag.
Plan around shuttle schedule; no campground access. Backup card (cashless).
Includes $25 meal card/person, souvenir poster, 1 locker, 1 welcome basket.

options.name: Good Life Tent Camping
options.lodging: BYO tent. Formerly Sherwood Court.
notes: Full BYO tent master kit (tent through cooler).
The A/C lounge + charging lounges reduce reliance on your own power, but you still bring full camp gear.

options.name: Good Life Village Car Camping
options.lodging: BYO tent beside your car, in the livelier Good Life Village core.
notes: Full master kit; pack heavier (car on-site).

options.name: Good Life Meadow Car Camping
options.lodging: BYO tent beside your car, quieter area.
notes: Identical to Village car camping; choose for quiet.
Bring everything; nothing extra provided beyond the car-side space.

options.name: Good Life Powered RV
options.lodging: BYO RV with 30A electrical + companion car.
notes: 30A cord/adapter + surge protector; self-contained fresh water and waste (powered, not full hookup); venue daypack.
No external generators.

options.name: Good Life Pre-Set RV
options.lodging: Furnished, powered Pre-Set RV, no vehicle needed (new for 2026): 30A, daily waste/water Thu–Sun, queen + bunks, full kitchen.
notes: Bring: food/groceries, drinking water beyond daily refill, your own coffee, personal toiletries, venue daypack.
The most hands-off RV option. Includes 1 car pass.

options.name: Electric Avenue
options.lodging: Furnished pre-set tent: full bedding for 2–4 twin or 1–2 queen; 2 side tables, 2 chairs, 1 bistro table, 2 lanterns, area rug; tent lock; single in-tent outlet. NO cooling unit, NO towels.
notes: A battery or USB fan is ESSENTIAL — there is NO cooling unit and the tents get hot (mesh limits airflow).
Bring your own bath/shower towels (NOT provided).
The in-tent outlet is rated only for charging small electronic devices — a fan + phone charging + a small light is fine, but do NOT run high-draw appliances (portable A/C, heaters).
Bring toiletries; cooler + food + stove; personal lighting/power bank.

options.name: Bungalow
options.lodging: Pre-set 4-person tent: 4 twin cots + 4 souvenir sleeping bags (yours to keep) + 4 pillows; 1 side table; 1 battery light; tent lock; single in-tent outlet. NO cooling unit, NO towels.
notes: Bring a fan — no cooling unit (tents run hot).
Bring your own towels (not provided).
Sheet/liner for cots if desired; extra lantern (only one battery light included).
Same outlet caveat as Electric Avenue — small devices only.
Bring cooler + food + stove; toiletries; power bank.

options.name: Back 40 Camping
options.lodging: BYO tent, rustic; permanent bathrooms + comp showers; 1 car pass.
notes: Full BYO tent master kit PLUS swim gear: swimsuit, towel, water shoes.
Old Western Town pool + 150-ft slide + hot tub + unlimited Frontier Water Park; daily brunch (Thu–Sun, 9 AM–1 PM ET) reduces breakfast-food needs.
Quick-dry towel for showers; separate beach towel for pool.

options.name: Back 40 FHU RV
options.lodging: BYO RV; full hook-up (30A + water + sewer); companion car.
notes: 30A cord/adapter + surge protector, sewer hose + fittings, drinking-water hose + pressure regulator.
Full hook-up means no water-rationing or waste-hauling. Food/groceries; swim gear; venue daypack.
No external generators (built-in/solar OK). Pool/waterpark + daily brunch.

options.name: Back 40 Cabin
options.lodging: Hard-sided A/C cabin: queen + 2 twin loft + queen sofa/futon; full private bathroom; kitchenette (microwave + mini-fridge); 2 parking passes.
notes: OPEN ITEM — EF is silent on whether linens/bedding and bath towels are included (unlike every other Good Life lodging type). The underlying Double JJ Resort cabins list "linens & towels provided," so likely supplied, but EF does not confirm in writing.
Kitchenette is microwave + mini-fridge only — no cooktop/oven, so a ≤1 lb camp stove is still needed for hot food; cookware/utensils are not confirmed.
Bring your own sheets/blankets/pillows and bath towels as backup, or confirm with the Good Life concierge (goodlife@electricforestfestival.com).
Also bring: groceries (kitchenette cooking), toiletries, swim gear, venue daypack.
Includes 2 Back 40 parking passes; pool/waterpark + daily brunch.

options.name: Back 40 Log Home
options.lodging: Large A/C log home (sleeps up to 8): king + double + 4 twin loft + queen sofa; 2 full baths; full kitchen + living room; 3 parking passes.
notes: SAME OPEN ITEM as the Cabin — EF is silent on linens/towels; the resort's log homes list "linens & towels provided," so likely supplied, but bring your own bedding/towels as backup or confirm with concierge.
Also bring: groceries/pantry staples + dish soap (resort units list cookware, but confirm), toiletries, swim gear, venue daypacks for the group.
Includes 3 Back 40 parking passes; pool/waterpark + daily brunch. Best option for large groups wanting a house.

options.name: Back 40 Enchanted Experience
options.lodging: Luxury pre-set tent with cooling unit; full bedding incl. woven throws + throw pillows; 2 side tables, bistro table, 2 chairs, 2 rugs, 2 lanterns, bench, mirror, trash can; bath towel sets + soap; in-tent outlet; dedicated golf-cart shuttle; spa lounge.
notes: Very little to bring. Personal clothing + costumes, toiletries beyond soap, swim gear, phone/power bank, venue daypack.
Food/snacks optional — or rely on brunch + vendors.
The lowest-effort camping option at the festival.

options.name: Good Life Hotel Package
options.lodging: Hotel room (linens, towels, A/C, housekeeping); 5 nights, Wed check-in; Good Life in-venue perks. ~Michillinda Lodge, Whitehall.
options.arrival: Check-in Wed June 24 / check-out Mon June 29.
notes: Venue daypack only + normal travel bag. Plan around shuttle; backup card (cashless).
Good Life perks: dedicated entrances, viewing areas, A/C lounge, happy hours.
Includes $25 meal card/person, poster, 1 locker, 1 welcome basket. Highest-comfort no-camping Good Life option.
